title: Sausage rolls
subtitle: Seasoned sausage meat wrapped in flaky rough-puff pastry
excerpt: Seasoned pork sausage meat encased in a quick rough-puff pastry, egg-washed and baked until deep golden and shatteringly flaky. Makes about 20.
sourceNotes: Sausage rolls in a flaky or puff pastry case are documented in British household baking guides from the mid-nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, includes sausage rolls among the pastry entries as a standard household savoury. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932), records them as a British tea-time and picnic staple. The rough-puff method is the home-baker's version of proper puff pastry, documented in both sources as producing a sufficiently flaky result for pies and rolls.

content.text: The pastry makes these. Bought puff will do the job, but rough-puff made at home takes twenty minutes of actual work and produces something noticeably flakier and more buttery. The trick is keeping the butter cold the whole time. The
form during folding, three turns is enough.

items.name: Unsalted butter
items.prepNote: cold, grated on the coarse side of a box grater

content.text: Mix the {{plain-flour}} and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a large bowl. Toss the grated {{unsalted-butter}} through the flour to coat the strands, do not rub in. Add 6 to 7 tbsp ice-cold water and mix with a knife until the dough just comes together in shaggy clumps. Turn out and press gently into a block. Roll to a long rectangle on a lightly floured surface. Fold the bottom third up, then the top third down (like a letter). Turn 90° and roll again. Repeat twice more. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Season the {{sausage-meat}} with the {{black-pepper}} and mix briefly. Roll the pastry to a large rectangle about 3 mm thick and cut into two long strips each about 12 cm wide. Divide the sausage meat between the strips and form into a log down the centre of each. Brush one long edge with beaten {{eggs}} and fold the pastry over to enclose the meat, pressing firmly to seal. Cut each roll into 5 cm lengths. Place seam-side down on lined baking trays. Brush with egg wash. Score the tops twice. Bake for 22 to 25 minutes until deep golden and the pastry has lifted and separated at the sides. Cool on a rack for 5 minutes before serving.
